title: Claude Code v2.1.143 (May 15) — plugin dependency enforcement, projected context cost in marketplace, worktree.bgIsolation setting, Opus 4.7 Fast Mode default (v2.1.142)
summary: v2.1.143: plugin dependency enforcement, projected context cost to /plugin marketplace browse pane, worktree.bgIsolation:none setting. v2.1.142: Fast mode now uses Opus 4.7 by default, new claude agents flags (--add-dir, --settings, --mcp-config, --plugin-dir, --permission-mode, --model, --effort), plugins with root-level SKILL.md now surfaced as a skill.

title: Claude Code v2.1.141 (May 13) — terminalSequence hook field, ANTHROPIC_WORKSPACE_ID, claude agents --cwd, Rewind Summarize
summary: v2.1.141: added terminalSequence field to hook JSON output for desktop notifications, ANTHROPIC_WORKSPACE_ID env var for workload identity federation, claude agents --cwd for scoping session list, Rewind menu 'Summarize up to here' option. v2.1.140: improved Agent tool subagent_type matching (case/separator-insensitive), updated agent color palette.

title: Agent SDK Credits: Anthropic reinstates OpenClaw + third-party agents — draws from separate credit pool (effective June 15)
summary: Anthropic reversed April ban on third-party agents (OpenClaw, etc.) by introducing 'Agent SDK credits' — a separate monthly pool per plan ($20 Pro / $100 Max 5x / $400 Max 20x). Starting June 15, OpenClaw draws from this pool, not interactive quota. Addresses capacity issues from unoptimized agents bypassing prompt caching.

title: Claude Code weekly limits +50% through July 13, 2026 — competitive response to Codex
summary: Anthropic raised Claude Code weekly limits by 50% through July 13 for Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise users. Third consecutive rate-limit improvement in 5 weeks (doubled 5-hour limits May 6 + removed peak hours + now weekly cap up). Explicit competitive move against OpenAI Codex.

title: Claude for Small Business — HN discussion (1 day ago)
summary: HackerNews community discussing onboarding non-software-engineer teammates to Claude Code, which has enhanced their ability to automate tasks. Active thread on Claude Code usability for non-technical users.

title: Zed 1.1.5 — fixed agent edits failing to apply, mouse cursor hide fix
summary: Zed stable release 1.1.5 fixes agent edits failing to apply in some cases, mouse cursor incorrectly hiding when Unsaved edits prompt appeared or when clicking panel buttons with search bars.

title: Cursor Bugbot switches to usage-based billing for Teams/Individual, opt-in before June 8
summary: Cursor switched Bugbot from seat-based to usage-based billing for Teams and Individual plans, removing seat fees and adding configurable effort levels for deeper PR reviews. Existing customers can opt in early; change takes effect at next renewal after June 8, 2026.
